narrator.description: A warm, friendly, and encouraging storyteller.

sunny.description: Cheerful, bright, and enthusiastic, like the sun.

cloudy.description: Gentle, calm, and wise, like an old cloud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Hello, brave explorer! Today, we will follow a tiny drop of water on its amazing journey. It travels all over Earth, helping plants and animals. Are you ready to begin our adventure?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Our little water drop starts in a big, blue ocean. The sun shines brightly on it. This journey is called the water cycle. A cycle means something that goes around and around, always repeating.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When the sun heats the water, something special happens.

segments.speaker: sunny
segments.text: Hello there, little water drop! I am Sunny, the Sun. I make you warm. When water gets warm enough, it changes from a liquid, like the water you drink, into a gas. This gas is called water vapor.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: This change, from liquid water to invisible gas, is called evaporation. The water vapor rises up, up into the sky.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.question_text: What happens to liquid water when the sun heats it up?
segments.acceptable_explanation: When the sun heats water, it changes into an invisible gas called water vapor. This process is called evaporation, and the water vapor floats up into the sky.
segments.hint_text: Remember what Sunny said! What did the water become when it got very warm?
fallback_choices: It turns into water vapor and goes up.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: That's exactly right! The water becomes vapor and floats high into the sky. Great job!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: That's okay! Let's remember. When the sun heats water, it changes into an invisible gas called water vapor. This vapor then floats high into the sky.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: As the water vapor rises higher, the air gets colder. It starts to get cozy with other water vapor particles.

segments.speaker: cloudy
segments.text: Greetings, little vapor! I am Cloudy, a wise old cloud. When water vapor gets cold, it changes back into tiny liquid water drops. This is called condensation. We all gather together to form a cloud!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: So, condensation is when water vapor in the air cools down and turns back into tiny liquid water droplets, forming clouds.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The clouds get bigger and bigger, holding more and more tiny water drops. When too many drops gather, they become heavy.

segments.speaker: cloudy
segments.text: Oh dear, I'm getting so full! When I can't hold any more water, it falls down to Earth as precipitation. Precipitation can be rain, snow, or even hail!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.question_text: What is one way water comes back down to Earth from the sky?
segments.acceptable_explanation: Water comes back down to Earth from the sky in different forms, such as rain, snow, sleet, or hail. This process is called precipitation.
segments.hint_text: Think about what happens when clouds get too heavy. What falls out of them?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: You got it! Rain is a perfect example of precipitation. The water then flows into rivers and lakes.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: That's okay! When clouds get too heavy, water falls back to Earth as precipitation. This can be rain, snow, or even hail. This water then flows into rivers.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: This water travels through rivers and streams, flowing back towards the big ocean. Sometimes it goes into the ground to become groundwater. This journey of evaporation, condensation, and precipitation keeps repeating. It's the amazing water cycle!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Wow, what an adventure! You followed a water drop from the ocean, up to the sky as vapor, into a cloud, and then back to Earth as rain. You learned about evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. You are a super water cycle explorer!
